title: Crotalaria iodina
intro: Crotalaria iodina, also known as Iodine Bush, is a flowering plant species in the legume family Fabaceae. Traditionally, Crotalaria iodina has been used in various cultures to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Crotalaria iodina is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, as well as to promote overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Crotalaria iodina is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of South America, specifically in the countries of Brazil and Argentina. The plant belongs to the genus Crotalaria, which comprises over 600 species of annual and perennial herbs, shrubs, and small trees. Crotalaria iodina is classified as a species of subgenus Crotalaria and section Crotalaria. Within the Fabaceae family, the plant is further classified into the subfamily Faboideae.

side_effects: Crotalaria iodina is known to be toxic and can cause severe side effects if ingested or if its sap comes into contact with the skin. Ingestion of the plant can lead to gastrointestinal problems, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can cause skin irritation, including redness, itching, and blistering.
